frameworks.name: psychophysics of subjective experience
frameworks.one_line: rigorous psychophysical methods applied to characterize subjective experience in dementia

frameworks.name: blindsight / visual awareness
frameworks.one_line: impoverished-experience framework from blindsight imported to AD/ADRD

frameworks.name: neural correlates of consciousness (association cortex)
frameworks.one_line: prefrontal and parietal NCC cited as potentially relevant substrate

frameworks.name: theories of consciousness
frameworks.one_line: explicitly rejected as premature and speculative for clinical AD work

core_question: How can subjective experience in Alzheimer's disease and related dementias be studied with the rigor of psychophysics?
key_assumptions: subjective experience can be probed behaviourally with psychophysical paradigms
blindsight's impoverished-experience characterization generalizes to AD/ADRD
association-cortex NCC may underlie perceptual consciousness deficits
interpretations: psychophysics borrowed from visual-awareness research is a productive route into AD/ADRD subjective experience
current theories of consciousness should be set aside as too speculative for this clinical purpose
limitations_noted: current consciousness theories are too premature and speculative to ground the program
